name: Chicken Fried Steak No. 1
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the round steak into slightly small portions and pound with a mallet or back of a knife until thin and all ragged.
2. Beat the eggs with a little water.
3. Mix the flour, salt, pepper, and about 1½ teaspoons of meat tenderizer together.
4. Dredge the meat through the eggs and then in the flour mixture.
5. Fry on medium-high heat in oil and butter. Be careful that it doesn't stick to the bottom of the pan too much. As you cook, sprinkle small bits of the flour mix into the pan so that you get a lot of good drippings for gravy.
6. When the meat is cooked through, drain on paper towels while making the gravy.
7. Pour off most of the oil from the pan, and add about ½ stick of butter or margarine.
8. Stir around over medium-low heat to scrape all the drippings off the bottom of the pan.
9. Sprinkle more of the flour mix into the pan until you almost have a roux.
10. Add milk a little bit at a time, stirring well with a flat spatula to get it all mixed up. When desired consistency is reached, remove from heat and serve with the steak.
11. Try serving with Black-eyed peas and lots of mashed potatoes or rice.
